content.assessment: T4 is comparatively the most deliverable proposal under this lens because it avoids new legal architecture, does not require resolving the institutional/programme misalignment problem, and can build on an already-existing non-structural lever (the 2023 teacher-career reform) rather than inventing a new delivery channel. Its principal implementation weakness is evaluative rather than mechanical: the proposal depends on being able to measure whether compensatory funding is working, and the evidence base documents a disputed statistics baseline between government and civil society that directly undermines the independent feedback loop this kind of incremental, evidence-adjusting policy requires.
opportunities: Being non-structural, this proposal is inherently pilotable at district or school-cohort level before national rollout
Could be paired with an independently verified data-collection protocol to partially resolve the measurement-baseline weakness
strengths: Does not require new legislation or restructuring of selection age, avoiding the coordination and veto-player exposure that structural proposals face
Builds on an existing, if unevaluated, policy channel (2023 teacher-career reform/subject incentive funding), reducing the actor-capacity burden of starting from zero
Hungary's low spending share and instructional time mean marginal reallocation is comparatively more consequential, improving the plausible cost-effectiveness of a targeted approach
threats: Persistence of tracking, free choice, and church-sector growth as parallel sorting mechanisms means measured equity gains could be structurally capped regardless of funding intensity, complicating attribution and evaluation
weaknesses: No described mechanism to resolve the disputed statistics baseline, which is a precondition for the credible monitoring and adaptation this incremental strategy depends on
Leaves classroom-level SES sorting untouched, meaning even successful school-level funding gains could be absorbed by within-school sorting with no described countermeasure
